reviews.review_title: Great Driving Feel, Needs More Content
reviews.review_description:
I think this game has a lot of potential. Being an Early Access title, it already has a solid foundation. Driving the car feels great, and the controls are smooth and enjoyable from the start.

There is still plenty of room for improvement. The UI works, but it could definitely use a visual redesign to make it more polished and appealing. The game also needs many more levels and additional ways to replay completed levels, which would add a lot more content and variety to the experience.

I really hope the developer continues to support and expand this project. As I mentioned before, it has a lot of potential, and with continued updates, I think it could turn into something really impressive.

I can't wait to see the future improvements. The potential is definitely there, and I hope I'll be able to update my review to 5 stars down the road.

I really wasn’t sure if I’d like this game. Really how exciting can parking a vehicle be? Let me tell you that this game is a real challenge. I haven’t made it through all the levels yet. And that’s because  - it’s very challenging..!

A super fun parking game 😁 Controls are really well made and to be I was pretty impressed by the graphic quality, I am really enjoying the game. It gets pretty tricky soon but the urge to finally complete a level made me come back. Overall a clear recommendation for a reasonable price Tag!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for the kind words! We’re really glad you’re enjoying the controls and visuals, it means a lot. The challenge definitely ramps up, but that “just one more try” feeling is exactly what we were going for.

reviews.review_description: This game nails the arcade-meets-realism vibe! The car handling is smooth and responsive, making it easy to pick up but still challenging—just like real parking. The mixed reality (MR) and VR modes work seamlessly, and the physics feel spot-on (especially the wheel-turning mechanics). Early access means room to grow, but the core gameplay is already solid. Perfect for quick, satisfying sessions. Highly recommend for fans of casual but skill-based games!

developer_response.body:
Really appreciate this. The goal was that arcade feel with just enough realism to make you question your own driving skills, so hearing it lands is honestly great. The MR and VR mix was a bit of a gamble, so it’s nice to know it’s working smoothly instead of setting people’s living rooms on fire.

We’re still early, but comments like this are basically fuel for the next updates. Thanks for taking the time to write it out.

reviews.review_description: I can see where the dev is going with this MR game.  It is still very early release and a concept trial, but shows a lot of promise.  The graphics are very clean and crisp.  The game play clear, drive a car, third person around obstacles, paths and in car parks slotting into a parking space.  The game only has 10 levels on stage one so clearly this will increase as the game develops.  It also would benefit from collision damage on the vehicles and walls and perhaps a timer to try and beat friends or yourself on how quickly you can park, pehaps points taken away from hitting things.  The price is too high for what it is but the devs are aware of this and I will adjust my review as the game updates.

developer_response.body:
Appreciate the detailed breakdown, this is exactly the kind of feedback that actually helps shape where the game goes instead of just “good game”

You’re right on the scope, it’s early access and stage one is just the foundation. More levels are already planned, and progression is meant to scale well beyond the current set.

Also agree on the gameplay depth ideas like collision feedback, timing challenges, and scoring systems. Those are already on our radar for upcoming updates since they fit the core loop really well and add that competitive layer without overcomplicating it.

And fair note on pricing, early access is always a weird balancing act between “support the build” and “this better come with 400 hours of content yesterday”.

Appreciate you taking the time to write this, it genuinely helps steer the direction.

reviews.review_description:
Firstly I was given a key for this game, but my opinion of it is my own.
The game looks first class, and controls intuitive and the graphics look amazing.
Currently 10 parking scenarios from easy to hard.
On the downside they’re are only 10 levels, I know there is more to come but the price seems a little to high for what there is at the moment. I enjoyed what I played but did not take too long to complete.
Looks promising going forward though.

developer_response.body:
Appreciate you taking the time to write this, especially since you mention you got a key, honesty like this is rare and actually useful.

Glad the visuals and controls landed well, that was a big focus from the start. And you’re right, it’s still early access, so the current set of levels is only the foundation of what’s coming next.

More content is already planned, along with deeper gameplay systems so it doesn’t stay in “nice demo I finished in one sitting” territory for long. Also fair point on pricing, early access always sits in that awkward space between supporting development and judging a half-built house.

Thanks again for the balanced feedback, it genuinely helps steer where things go next.

reviews.review_description:
I’ve had the pleasure in getting this game for free (won in a prize giveaway). A huge thank you to the developers for that 🫶🏻. 

The game is in the early days of development. But is very addictive to play. The overall look is very good. 

Few things as a suggestion. 

Be able to move the playing area around ( grab it and make it bigger or smaller up and down ) ￼
Don’t know if you can make it so you can sit in the car and drive it. (Might be a future thing) 
Could have a League  table for the fastest times 
Multiplayer mode so you can race against people. 

Looking forward to more content and updates. 

￼

reviews.review_title: A new genre for me, fun in mixed reality
reviews.review_description:
I received this as a prize. I've never played this style of "parking simulator" game genre before but it's certainly worth a look especially if you enjoy controlling miniatures in mixed reality.
Vehicle models are great, I can see inside the interiors and reflections glint off the paint and glass. Vehicle physics are well done and surprising to see in a game of this type. There's a visible shift in vehicle weight during accelleration and braking as well as body roll when doing a handbrake turn.

I've only played about 4 levels so far but it's proving fun/challenging.

Some issues and suggestions:

1) Add some tyre squeal sounds and tyre marks when doing a handbrake turn.
2) Instruction screen text is cut off at far right of image.
3) Unable to grab vehicles as indicated in instructions.
4) I would like to be able to place the scene on my floor, not just at table height. Took me a little while to realise I can grab the "handle" block and adjust scene up and down a little but can't push it to the floor.
5) Ability to scale the scene to various size, perhaps by holding in both grip triggers and moving controllers closer together/apart.
6) I would like to be able to drive outside the scene in mixed reality and onto my floor. Perhaps the scene edges could blend with MR a little better by creating a feathered transparent edge.
7) Unsure if parallel parking is possible in later levels? If not then consider adding as a feature.
8) Ability for player to add rain to a scene. This would make it more slippery and thus change the physics of the handbrake turn allowing for power slides into parallel parks (similar to the real world extreme parking sport).
9) Add loose gravel parking lots levels, this would change the physics and difficulty. Particle/Dust effects on these levels.
10) Ghost runs against your previous best time for a level. Showing previous path as a ghost vehicle.

Well done to the developer, looking forward to updates.

Wow, amazing mechanics and game feel.

When are you planning to release new levels?

developer_response.body:
Glad the mechanics are hitting the mark, always a relief when physics behave like they were invited to the project instead of freeloading.

New levels are already in development and will be rolling out in updates soon. We’re focusing on expanding variety and difficulty progression so it keeps that “one more try” energy without turning into frustration simulator 2026.

reviews.review_description: Seems like a simple idea but it's very fun and addictive to play!

developer_response.body:
Thanks man, we are aiming for even better gameplay. 

#Nostalgic

reviews.review_description: I like a lot of things in this game and after playing for a while it became more and more fun.  It takes some getting used to the controls.  Can’t wait to experience the final version.

reviews.review_title: A fun game with an interesting premise
reviews.review_description:
You have to park the cars and vans. Easy, right? Maybe not so easy. 

This is a fairly simplistic game but the difficulty increases at a steady pace. 

It’s a little janky in places. The models for the cars and vans all look great, but the in VR background of a plain blue sky is a little dull. Maybe give it a living room or hangout background and make the game look like a tabletop RC game. 

The physics on the cars are great, but the vans feel a little off. I also feels some work could be done to make the menu’s look a little more premium. The cars and levels they drive on all look great, but small touches will bring this game up even more. 

I’d be interested to see if these developers could create a Micro Machines style racing game with these models and the table top style base. 

This game is worth trying though for sure.

developer_response.body:
Really appreciate the breakdown though, it’s actually useful feedback buried under polite destruction.
Background point is fair. The current skybox is placeholder level “we promise we’re working on it,” so the tabletop RC idea is actually aligned with where we’re heading visually. That kind of framing would also help the whole scale feel more intentional instead of empty VR void.
Physics note on vans is also valid, heavier vehicles are still getting tuned so they don’t feel like slightly depressed cars.
Menus being more premium is 100 percent on the list, VR UI is one of those things that looks simple until you try to make it not feel like a floating spreadsheet.
And yeah, the Micro Machines style idea is not exactly new territory for us mentally, it’s one of those “dangerously obvious” directions that keeps popping up for a reason.
Glad you still found it worth trying, even while gently pointing out everything that could be better.
